skill.name: angular
skill.description: Modern Angular guidance for AI agents, covering Signals, standalone components, zoneless change detection, routing, SSR, hydration, forms, testing, and migration patterns.
skill.summary: Guide Angular development with modern v20+ patterns, Signals, standalone components, SSR, hydration, and migration advice.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium findings, but review found they are documentation examples and reference links rather than executable skill code. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, command execution path, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Reference URLs and HttpClient Examples Are Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Angular documentation or placeholder sample APIs. The HttpClient calls are inside Angular tutorial snippets, not executable marketplace tooling, so this is a false positive for network exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are reference lists or Angular code examples. No code path in the skill sends local data, secrets, or user content to a remote endpoint.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Angular Lazy Loading Examples
low_findings.description: The import() calls demonstrate Angular route lazy loading for components and route files. They use static relative module paths in documentation examples and do not load remote or user-controlled code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic imports are standard Angular route examples with fixed local paths. There is no evidence of arbitrary module loading or remote script execution.

low_findings.title: External Command Matches Are Markdown and TypeScript Syntax
low_findings.description: The static command findings come from markdown code fences, inline code, TypeScript templates, testing examples, and Angular API names. No shell, Ruby, child process, or command runner implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are prose or TypeScript samples. The repository contains no executable script file or function that invokes external commands.

low_findings.title: High Severity Scanner Matches Are Benign Angular Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, memory-mapped file access, and system reconnaissance alerts match unrelated substrings such as description text, computed values, includes(), describe(), and input.required(). They do not represent those behaviors.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows Angular prose or normal test and component examples. No cryptographic API, mmap API, or reconnaissance command is present.

content.user_title: Build Modern Angular Apps with Signals
content.value_statement: Angular teams need current guidance for Signals, standalone components, SSR, hydration, and zoneless patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Angular v20+ patterns for implementation and migration work.
actual_capabilities: Explains Angular Signals, computed values, effects, signal inputs, outputs, and model bindings.
Shows standalone component setup, provider configuration, routing, lazy loading, guards, and resolvers.
Guides zoneless Angular setup and change detection patterns for modern applications.
Covers SSR, hydration, deferred loading, and performance-focused Angular architecture.
Provides migration guidance from NgModules and legacy Angular patterns to modern APIs.
Includes testing patterns for standalone components and signal-based inputs.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not execute Angular CLI commands or modify projects by itself.
It focuses on Angular v20+ and may not fit older projects that cannot upgrade.
Some future-facing Angular features may require version checks before production use.
It does not replace official Angular release notes for exact API stability details.

use_cases.title: Plan a Signals Migration
use_cases.description: Convert selected component state and derived values from legacy patterns to Angular Signals with a staged plan.

use_cases.title: Create a Standalone Feature Area
use_cases.description: Design a new routed Angular feature with standalone components, lazy loading, route guards, and typed services.

use_cases.title: Improve Angular Runtime Performance
use_cases.description: Review an Angular app for zoneless readiness, hydration issues, deferred loading opportunities, and change detection problems.

prompt_templates.title: Explain the Modern Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the angular skill to explain how Signals and standalone components should be used in a new Angular v20+ feature.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the angular skill to refactor this Angular component toward Signals, signal inputs, computed values, and standalone imports. Explain each change briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Routed Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the angular skill to design a lazy-loaded Angular feature with standalone components, functional guards, resolvers, and typed service boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the angular skill to review this Angular migration plan for Signals, zoneless change detection, SSR, hydration, testing risk, and rollout order.

output_examples.input: Review a legacy Angular component that stores derived state manually.
output_examples.output: A concise migration review that identifies state candidates for Signals, recommends computed values, and lists tests needed after refactoring.

output_examples.input: Plan a new admin dashboard route in Angular v20+.
Find performance issues in an Angular page.
output_examples.output: A prioritized checklist covering change detection, deferred loading, signal usage, hydration consistency, and network data loading patterns.

best_practices: Verify the project Angular version before applying version-specific APIs.
Migrate incrementally and protect each step with focused component and integration tests.
Use official Angular documentation to confirm preview or experimental feature status.
anti_patterns: Do not force zoneless mode into a legacy app without testing change detection behavior.
Do not replace every Observable with Signals when the data is event-driven or asynchronous.
Do not use future-facing examples as production code without checking API stability.

faq.question: What Angular versions does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets modern Angular v20+ patterns, with emphasis on Signals, standalone components, SSR, hydration, and zoneless applications.

faq.question: Can this skill run Angular CLI commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples only. The user or agent must run project commands separately.

faq.question: Does it support legacy NgModule applications?
faq.answer: It can help plan migration from NgModules, but it is optimized for modern standalone Angular architecture.

faq.question: Is it useful for SSR and hydration work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes SSR, hydration, deferred loading, and consistency concerns for modern Angular applications.

faq.question: Does it replace official Angular documentation?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes practical patterns and should be paired with official documentation for exact API details.

faq.question: Why does the audit show low risk?
faq.answer: The reviewed risk markers are documentation examples and reference links. No executable malicious behavior was found.
